81 passed, 2 failed; both failures environmental, not regressions: viewport test passes in isolation (was a poisoned-ENV_MUTEX cascade); same-origin test dies at socket fixture (path 161 > SUN_LEN 104, test bakes 63-char fn-name as socket filename under CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR); both tests untouched since base; diff touches no origin/relay logic; upstream branch ctate/fix-flaky-stream-viewport-test confirms pre-existing fragility

findings.claim: When a client loosens its own frame-rate cap mid-stream (e.g. maxFps 1 -> 0/uncapped, or 1 -> 30), the next frame is delayed by up to the previous frame interval. The reader task stores the new max_fps in the atomic but has no channel to wake the writer, which stays blocked in sleep_until(next_allowed) on the stale deadline computed from the old (slower) rate.
findings.evidence: Forced empirically: scratch test set maxFps=1, sent+received a frame (setting next_allowed=+1s), sent maxFps=0, then a new frame arrived in 850ms instead of immediately (assert <200ms failed). Reverted after. Only affects the transition when loosening; tightening (30->1) is sound; the uncapped-from-start and set-once-at-connect paths are unaffected. Transient one-frame stall, no data loss, no crash.
findings.layer: writer select! timer arm / config-change propagation to the writer loop (websocket.rs:286-306)
findings.path: reader_loop stores max_fps (websocket.rs ~354) -> writer sleep_until(next_allowed) never re-evaluated while blocked (websocket.rs:292)

exemptions.evidence: The maxFps/latest-first feature is a WebSocket client->server protocol message, not a CLI command/flag/env-var; the three stream MCP tools (mcp.rs:1604-1618) are one-line declarations describing the CLI subcommand and carry no protocol semantics to sync. Verified by reading mcp.rs:1585-1645.

The core skill has never documented the WS streaming protocol (grep: only AGENT_BROWSER_STREAM_PORT in references/commands.md); it teaches CLI usage to agents, while frame delivery is a programmatic integration surface documented in README + docs site.

exemptions.claim: Intermediate frames are dropped (old ordered broadcast delivered all frames up to lag)
exemptions.evidence: Only live preview consumes WS frames; video recording is CDP-side screencast-to-file, independent of the WS broadcast. Dropping intermediate frames is the intended tradeoff for latest-frame-wins and affects no recording path.

issue_candidates.title: e2e_stream_command_requires_same_origin_before_daemon_relay unrunnable on macOS due to socket path length
issue_candidates.evidence: Test builds the UDS socket path as CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/target/t/<tmp>/<63-char-fn-name>.sock = 161 chars > macOS SUN_LEN 104; fails at fixture bind on any moderately long repo path (main worktree ~138 chars too).
issue_candidates.why_out_of_scope: Untouched test, pre-existing (upstream ctate/fix-flaky-stream-viewport-test branch), unrelated to this diff's frame-delivery/input changes.

issue_candidates.title: Poisoned ENV_MUTEX cascades one test failure into unrelated tests
issue_candidates.evidence: test_utils.rs:15 ENV_MUTEX.lock().unwrap() panics with PoisonError when a prior EnvGuard-holding test panics; the same-origin socket panic cascaded into e2e_stream_frame_metadata_respects_custom_viewport, which passes cleanly in isolation.
issue_candidates.why_out_of_scope: Test-harness fragility, not diff behavior; would be closed by .lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) in EnvGuard.
